content.description:
# App Pages and Widgets

[App Pages](item://c4563df8-2eb4-4ad6-9502-5bf35b28e410) are grid-based interactive pages composed from widget items. They use Hobson's existing item and attachment infrastructure — a page is an item, each widget is an item, and the page holds widgets as attachments with grid positioning.

## Architecture

The system uses three existing mechanisms:

1. **Attachments array** — widget items are attachments of the page item, exactly as spatial container children are
2. **Per-attachment view configuration** — the `view` object on each attachment stores grid position (`col`, `row`, `colSpan`, `rowSpan`) rather than spatial coordinates
3. **View rendering** — each widget is rendered by its own view, resolved through the standard preference hierarchy

This is architecturally analogous to the spatial container, but with a CSS grid layout model and a shared state mechanism for inter-widget communication.

### Items

| Item | Type | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `app-page` | type-def | The page type (`c4563df8-2eb4-4ad6-9502-5bf35b28e410`) |
| `app-page-view` | view | Renders grid, manages shared state, design mode (`ef977316-0496-487a-b662-0ba07e40eea2`) |
| `app-page-design-lib` | library | Design mode UI logic (`9e0953dc-b75c-4015-9c6f-79afad34d5a1`) |
| `widget-markdown` | type-def | Markdown text block |
| `widget-button` | type-def | Clickable button with inline code |
| `widget-radio-group` | type-def | Single-selection radio buttons |
| `widget-checkbox-group` | type-def | Checkboxes populated from a code query |
| `note-widget-view` | view | Compact markdown render of notes for page embedding (`b7f3a1d2-8e5c-4f6a-9d0b-1c2e3f4a5b6c`) |
| `page-widget` | tag | Tag on type-defs that appear in the Create New widget picker (`c0c0c0c0-0071-0000-0000-000000000000`) |

Each widget type has a corresponding `*-view` item. New widget types can be added incrementally (one type-def + one view) without modifying any existing code.

## Page Data Model

An app-page item has:

- `content.columns` (number, default 12) — grid column count
- `content.gap` (number, default 8) — gap between cells in pixels
- `content.rowHeight` (number, default 40) — fixed row height in pixels. Uses `grid-auto-rows` with `row-gap: 0`; vertical spacing comes from cell padding. Widgets use `rowSpan` to occupy multiple rows. Cells have `overflow: auto` so tall content scrolls within its allocated space

Attachments use the standard `{ id, view }` format with grid-specific view properties:

```
{ id: "<widget-id>", view: { col: 1, row: 1, colSpan: 12, rowSpan: 1, viewId?: "<view-guid>" } }
```

All positions are 1-based, matching CSS Grid convention. The optional `viewId` field overrides the standard view resolution for this attachment, allowing a specific view to be used without changing the item's global preferred view.

## Shared State (pageContext)

Widgets communicate through a runtime key-value state object maintained by the page view. This state is **not persisted** — it exists only while the page is rendered.

The page view creates a `pageContext` object and passes it to widget views via an augmented API (`Object.create(api)` with `pageContext` added). Widget views access it through `api.pageContext`.

### API

- `pageContext.getState(key)` — read a value
- `pageContext.setState(key, value)` — write a value and notify listeners
- `pageContext.onStateChange(key, callback)` — subscribe to changes; returns unsubscribe function

### How Widgets Use State

**Producers** (radio groups, checkbox groups) write to state via their `stateKey` content field:
```javascript
pageContext.setState(widget.content.stateKey, selectedValue);
```

**Consumers** (buttons) read state in their `content.code`:
```javascript
const selected = pageContext.getState('selectedItems');
```

Widgets that don't use `pageContext` work fine standalone — they're just normal views.

## Widget Types

### widget-markdown

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `body` | string | Markdown content to render |

Uses `hobson-markdown` library with fallback to `<pre>` if unavailable.

### widget-button

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `label` | string | Button text |
| `variant` | string | `primary`, `secondary`, or `danger` |
| `code` | string | JavaScript executed on click with `api` and `pageContext` in scope |

Shows "Working..." during execution and displays errors inline.

### widget-radio-group

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `label` | string | Group heading |
| `options` | array | `[{ value, label }]` objects |
| `defaultValue` | string | Initially selected value |
| `stateKey` | string | Key to write selection to in page state |
| `layout` | string | `vertical` (default) or `horizontal` |

### widget-checkbox-group

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `label` | string | Group heading |
| `source` | string | JavaScript that returns an array (has `api` in scope) |
| `labelField` | string | Dot-path field on source items to use as label |
| `stateKey` | string | Key to write selected IDs to in page state |

## Design Mode

The app-page-view includes a visual design mode for editing layout without manually editing JSON. A small "Design" button in the top-right corner of the page toggles between modes.

### Live Mode

Widgets are fully interactive. No design UI is visible (except the toggle button).

### Design Mode

A toolbar appears with:
- **Live Mode** button — switch back to live mode
- **+ Add Widget** button — opens a picker listing all `widget-*` type-defs
- Grid settings display (column count, gap, row height)

Each widget shows:
- **Header bar** with drag handle, widget name, position label (`c1 r2 12×3`), edit button, delete button
- **Content** rendered but non-interactive (`pointer-events: none`)
- **Resize handle** at bottom-right corner for changing `colSpan` and `rowSpan`
- **Selection border** on click

### Design Operations

| Operation | Mechanism |
|-----------|----------|
| **Reposition** | Drag the handle; floating label shows target `col, row`; persisted via `updateSilent` |
| **Resize** | Drag bottom-right corner; `colSpan` and `rowSpan` update live; position label shows both dimensions during drag |
| **Edit properties** | Opens the item in its standard editable view via `api.navigate()` |
| **Add widget** | Two-tab modal: **Attach Existing** searches all items (reuses `item-search-lib`); **Create New** lists type-defs tagged with `page-widget`. Attaching a note auto-sets `viewId` to `note-widget-view` for compact rendering |
| **Remove widget** | Three-way modal: Remove from Page (detach only), Delete Permanently (`api.delete`), or Cancel |

All design operations use the `updateSilent` pattern (fresh-read → modify attachments → save) to avoid race conditions. The page always fresh-reads on re-render so design changes are immediately visible in both modes.

### Design Mode Implementation

Design logic lives in `app-page-design-lib` (loaded only in design mode) to keep the view code manageable. It exports:

- `renderToolbar(pageItem, api, callbacks)` — the design toolbar
- `wrapWidgetForDesign(widgetElement, childSpec, childItem, pageItem, api, callbacks)` — design overlay per widget
- `renderColumnOverlay(gridEl, columns)` — semi-transparent column lines
- `showWidgetPicker(api)` — two-tab modal (Attach Existing / Create New) returning `{ mode, typeId|item }` or `null`
- `showPropertyEditor` — removed; edit button now uses `api.navigate()` to open the item in its standard view

## Creating a New Widget Type

1. Create a **type-definition** item named `widget-<name>`. Document content fields in the description using the `- \`fieldName\` (type)` format (the property editor parses this to seed forms for new instances).
2. Create a **view** item named `widget-<name>-view` with `for_type` pointing to the type-def. Export an async `render(item, api)` function. Access shared state via `api.pageContext` if needed.
3. Set `preferredView` on the type-def to point to the view.

4. Tag the type-def with `page-widget` (`c0c0c0c0-0071-0000-0000-000000000000`) so it appears in the Create New widget picker.

To make any existing type embeddable on a page without creating a dedicated widget type, use the **Attach Existing** tab in the picker. For notes, the page automatically assigns `note-widget-view` which renders the markdown body compactly without the standard note chrome.
